era: 1958-present. Surf Rock / Instrumental. E=5 -- clean, reverb-drenched, melodic guitar. Tacoma, Washington.

location.origin: Tacoma, Washington (FIRST TACOMA BAND IN ENGINE)

members: Leon Taylor (drums, b. 1966, son of Mel)

theory_nodes.concept: Walk Don't Run (1960): Johnny Smith composition via Chet Atkins, transformed into electric rock. #2 Billboard pop chart. 200+ cover versions. The most-covered instrumental rock song. Melodic clarity + clean production = infinite interpretive flexibility.
theory_nodes.description: Walk Don't Run as the defining surf rock instrumental.

theory_nodes.concept: Japan cultural impact: Ventures guitar boom 1960s. Tens of thousands of Japanese musicians learned electric guitar via Ventures songbooks. 30-50 million records sold in Japan. Originary influence on Japanese rock equivalent to Beatles on American rock.
theory_nodes.description: The Ventures as the foundation of Japanese electric guitar culture.

theory_nodes.concept: FIRST TACOMA BAND in engine. 32 miles south of Seattle; port city, working-class character, distinct identity. Don Wilson (used car salesman) + Bob Bogle (construction worker) formed the band in a parking lot. Working-class Pacific Northwest origin.
theory_nodes.description: Tacoma enters the engine for the first time.

theory_nodes.concept: E=5 surf rock: clean tone (no distortion), reverb (bright, notes sustaining), tremolo (amplitude modulation, watery quality). Fender guitars -- Stratocaster, Jaguar. The anti-grunge sound of the Pacific Northwest. Same region, opposite aesthetic.
theory_nodes.description: Surf rock's acoustic signature in the engine's energy framework.

cross_references.relationship: E=6 to E=5. Eastlake to Tacoma. First non-Seattle entry in engine.
Garage Rock, 1960 formation. Adjacent Pacific Northwest 1960s rock. Columbia Records at 240 contrasts with Dolton at 239.

sps_pair.pairing_note: Metallic clean jump (E=5) + metallic clean guitar tone (E=5). Both cross open water: Steelhead is anadromous (ocean + river); Ventures crossed the Pacific culturally to build Japan audience. E=5+5=10. Equilibrium.

milestones: Largest global sales of any Pacific Northwest act by units
